generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Primary actuator for temperature regulation using bang-bang control strategy (on/off cycling) with UL (Upper Limit) - LL (Lower Limit) range (e.g., 20°C - 30°C). Safety protocol enforced in low-level driver to prevent overheating."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Distributes heat uniformly inside styrofoam box via RabbitMQ commands from controller for thermal regulation."
]

instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Type: Bang-bang controller with statechart-based logic (Figure 8). Parameters: LL (Lower Limit), UL (Upper Limit), H (Heating Duration), C (Waiting Duration). Communicates via RabbitMQ with low-level driver for real-time adjustment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LowLevelRabbitMQDriver
]

instance LowLevelRabbitMQDriver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Protocol: RabbitMQ for bidirectional communication with controller. Safety protocol enforces safe states (e.g., preventing unsafe temperature spikes). Periodically uploads PT states (fan/heatbed status) to RabbitMQ."
    DTDFVocab:enables BangBangController
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution temperature sensor (±0.1°C precision) monitoring internal temperature in styrofoam box at critical points (center/edges). Implied high-frequency sampling (1Hz or higher)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempDataStream
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution temperature sensor (±0.1°C precision) monitoring internal temperature in styrofoam box at secondary critical points. Implied high-frequency sampling (1Hz or higher)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempDataStream
]

instance ExternalAmbientSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors ambient temperature outside styrofoam box with lower sampling frequency (implied ~0.1Hz)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTempDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTempDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTempDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalAmbientSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified 2-parameter thermal model for temperature prediction (generalized but less accurate for heatbed inertia)."
]

instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enhanced 4-parameter model better predicting heatbed thermal inertia (critical for dynamic scenarios). Used for Kalman filtering state estimation."
]

instance TemperatureSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings from internal/external sensors (internal: ±0.1°C precision, external: ambient context)."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTempDataStream, ExternalTempDataStream
]

instance KalmanFilterOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Estimated heatbed temperature states derived from Model B and sensor measurements via Kalman filter."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterImplementation
]

instance ControllerParameterData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) for bang-bang control optimization via co-simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoSimulationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker for real-time synchronization between controller, low-level driver, and DT components. Enables asynchronous event-driven communication (AMQP protocol)."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded system (Raspberry Pi) implementing bang-bang control logic with statechart-based parameters (LL, UL, H, C)."
    DTDFVocab:enables BangBangController
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Database storing real-time/historical temperature data for visualization and anomaly detection (future implementation)."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance CoSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation tools (Python-based frameworks) for on-demand calibration and what-if scenarios using historical/real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:enables WhatIfSimulationService, CalibrationService
]

instance KalmanFilterImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation tool integrating sensor data with Model B predictions for heatbed temperature estimation."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

// C12: Data Transmission Protocols
instance RabbitMQProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-latency, reliable message passing protocol for periodic sensor data uploads (implied ~seconds/minutes intervals) via low-level driver."
    DTDFVocab:enables LowLevelRabbitMQDriver
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time visualization dashboards with time-series temperature trends via low-level driver messages and RabbitMQ communication."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDistributionInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detects deviations from expected behavior using Kalman filter correlating sensor data with Model B predictions (future implementation)."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyTriggeredAction, ControllerPerformanceInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated calibration of Model B parameters via historical/real-time data tracking simulator (Document 5)."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelRecalibrationAction
]

instance WhatIfSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Co-simulation with historical/real-time PT data to optimize controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) via statechart analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance SelfAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop process for dynamic controller reconfiguration: anomaly detection → experiment scheduling → model calibration → parameter optimization."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelRecalibrationAction, SimulationValidationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureDistributionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted temperature distribution inside styrofoam box using Model B's focus on heatbed thermal inertia."
]

instance ControllerPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Evaluation of bang-bang controller effectiveness (LL, UL, H, C parameters) via simulation-based validation."
]

instance AnomalyTriggeredAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic response to temperature deviations (e.g., cold water added) triggering cooling/heating cycles and model recalibration."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of controller parameters (H, C) based on real-time sensor feedback and DT simulations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelRecalibrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated update of Model B parameters after environmental changes (e.g., adding new materials) via tracking simulator."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SimulationValidationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Running what-if scenarios to validate controller behavior before deployment using historical/real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalAmbientSensor
]


instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Command Types**:
  - **Heating commands**: Activate/deactivate the heatbed (via control signals).
  - **Fan commands**: Activate/deactivate the fan (via control signals).

  **Control Message Formats**:
  - Commands are sent via **RabbitMQ** (message broker) to the low-level driver.
  - No explicit format specification (e.g., JSON/AMQP message payloads), but implied to be structured for actuator control.

  **Validation & Authentication**:
  - No explicit mention of validation/authentication mechanisms; assumed minimal checks (e.g., safe protocol in low-level driver to prevent unsafe states).

  **Feedback Systems**:
  - Controller receives temperature measurements from sensors via RabbitMQ.
  - **Bang-bang control logic** (statechart-based) regulates temperature with parameters:
    - **LL**: Lower limit for temperature.
    - **UL**: Upper limit for temperature.
    - **H**: Heating duration.
    - **C**: Waiting duration (delay after actuation).

  **Conflict Resolution**:
  - No explicit conflict resolution; assumed implicit in the **safe protocol** of the low-level driver to prevent unsafe states.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    - **Sensor Data Update Frequency**: Periodic reading of temperature data by the low-level driver (Document 6) is implied but not quantified. No explicit time interval is provided.
    - **Real-time Visualization**: Goal mentioned in Document 5 for real-time dashboard updates, but no specific latency or update frequency is defined.
    - **On-demand Calibration**: Document 5 describes automatic calibration as a process triggered by anomalies, but no temporal constraints or intervals are specified.
    - **Latency Requirements**: Not explicitly mentioned; inferred as minimal for anomaly detection and controller adjustments, but no concrete values provided.
    - **Temporal Granularity**: No granularity specified beyond periodic sensor readings (assumed to be in the order of seconds or less, inferred from typical CPS sensor update rates).
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    - **Architecture Pattern**:
      - **CPS-Based Digital Twin Framework** (Document 1, 4):
        - Integrates **models (A/B)**, **data streams (sensors)**, **algorithms (Kalman filter, self-adaptation loop)**, and **optimization** (what-if simulations).
        - **Calibration Loop**: Uses **algorithm-driven updates** (Document 1) to refine models (A/B) with real-time PT data.
        - **Control Loop**: Combines **PT monitoring** (sensors) with **DT predictions** (models) for closed-loop regulation (Document 5).
      - **Component Integration**:
        - **Hardware-Digital Interface**:
          - **Low-level driver** abstracts PT hardware (sensors/actuators) and forwards data to RabbitMQ (Document 6).
          - **Controller** (bang-bang logic) communicates via RabbitMQ to the DT (Document 6).
        - **Software Stack**:
          - **RabbitMQ**: Message broker for PT-DT synchronization (Document 6).
          - **Time-series Database**: Stores PT data for visualization (Document 5).
          - **Co-simulation Environment**: Enables real-time/what-if simulations (Document 5).
      - **Resource Management**:
        - **Single-Node Deployment**: All components (Raspberry Pi + RabbitMQ) run on a **single physical machine** (Document 6).
        - **Scalability**: RabbitMQ supports **distributed scaling** (Document 6) but not explicitly used in this case study.
      - **System-Wide Coordination**:
        - **Self-Adaptation Loop** (Document 5):
          - **Autonomous Reconfiguration**: DT dynamically adjusts models, filters, and controllers based on PT anomalies.
          - **Data-Driven Calibration**: Model B is recalibrated after new experiments (Document 5).
        - **Optimization Integration**: What-if simulations inform controller parameter tuning (Document 5).
  "
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    **Development Stages:**
    - **Conceptual Phase:** Abstract representation of the incubator system (e.g., CAD-like models for physical properties like styrofoam box dimensions, heatbed placement, and sensor locations).
    - **Modeling Phase:**
      - Creation of **two dynamic thermal models** (Model A: 2 free parameters; Model B: 4 free parameters) for temperature prediction inside the styrofoam box, accounting for thermal inertia of the heatbed.
      - Models are calibrated using real-time sensor data (Document 3, 5).
    - **Integration Phase:**
      - Models are integrated into a **Digital Twin (DT)** framework, combining with data (sensor readings) and algorithms (e.g., Kalman filter for state estimation, supervisory controllers).
      - DT enables real-time monitoring, calibration, and anomaly detection (Document 5).
    - **Operational Phase:**
      - DT runs continuously, providing **real-time visualization** (time-series database + dashboard), **on-demand calibration**, and **self-adaptation loops** (e.g., reconfiguring the controller when environmental changes occur, like adding cold water).
      - DT supports **what-if simulations** for controller parameter optimization (Document 5).

    **Lifecycle Governance:**
    - **Open-source hardware/software** (GitHub repository) ensures reproducibility and community-driven evolution (Document 2, 3).
    - **Version control** implied by open-source practices (e.g., GitHub) for tracking model updates and DT evolution.
    - **Transition Management:** Models evolve from simplified (Model A) to refined (Model B) as thermal dynamics are better understood, enabling iterative DT calibration and adaptation.
  "
]

    base:desc "
  **Hosting Platforms**:
  - **Edge deployment**: Raspberry Pi (on-premises) for local control and data processing.
  - **Cloud/On-premises RabbitMQ**: Deployed as a central message broker (lightweight, scalable).

  **Computational Requirements**:
  - **Raspberry Pi**: Low-power, embedded device for real-time control (e.g., ARM Cortex-A72, ~1GHz).
  - **RabbitMQ**: Lightweight broker (no heavy computational overhead).

  **Storage Requirements**:
  - No explicit storage details; assumed minimal storage for sensor logs (future goal: time-series database).

  **Scalability Mechanisms**:
  - RabbitMQ supports horizontal scaling (multiple brokers for load balancing).
  - No explicit mention of distributed DT components; assumed single-node setup for simplicity.

  **Deployment Models**:
  - **Monolithic**: Low-level driver, controller, and RabbitMQ deployed as a cohesive system.
  - **Modular**: Components (e.g., controller, data visualization) can be extended independently (future goals).

  **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
  - No redundancy; assumed single-point failure risk (e.g., RabbitMQ instance crash).
  - **Safe protocol** in low-level driver ensures safe operation even with failures.
  "
]

    base:desc "

        - **Kalman Filter**: Implemented as a **custom algorithm** (Document 5) for heatbed state estimation.
    - **Dependencies on External Systems**:

        - **Sensors/Actuators**: Directly coupled to the PT (styrofoam box, heatbed, fan).

      - **Software Dependencies**:

        - **Time-Series Database**: Implied but unspecified (likely **InfluxDB or similar** for visualization).
        - **Co-simulation Tools**: Custom or generic (e.g., MATLAB/Simulink for Model A/B).
    - **Integration Patterns with Other DTs/Enterprise Systems**:

      - **Potential Extensions**:
        - Could integrate with **cloud-based DT platforms** (e.g., AWS IoT, Azure Digital Twins) via RabbitMQ or REST APIs.
        - Could use **standardized protocols** (e.g., OPC UA) for interoperability with other CPS DTs.
  "
]

        - **Threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., anomaly detection for sensor spoofing).

      - **FMI-enabled data broker (RabbitMQ FMU)** referenced in Document 2 (not applied in the incubator case study).
